title: Health worker monitored for Ebola in London hospital after working in DRC
description:
UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease

A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus.

The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said.

Continue reading...
summary: A UK resident who worked in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is being monitored for Ebola in a London hospital after potential exposure to the virus. The individual was medically evacuated from the DRC, where they were treating patients with the disease. The UK Health Security Agency is overseeing the situation.

title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
description:
Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well

One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.

There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned.

Continue reading...
summary: A new UN report found that one in three people globally cannot afford a healthy diet, with 2.69 billion people unable to meet their energy and nutrient needs. This highlights the urgent need for action on food affordability to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030.

title: São Tomé and Príncipe heads to polls in tense presidential election
description:
Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent

Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials.

According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora.

Continue reading...
summary: São Tomé and Príncipe is holding a presidential election on Sunday, with incumbent Carlos Vila Nova seeking a second term as an independent candidate. The election aims to strengthen the country's democratic credentials. Approximately 142,000 people are registered to vote, with 15% living abroad.

title: ‘We are preserving a tradition’: how Ghana’s sensationalist film posters became collectible art
description:
Hand-painted works are often wildly unfaithful to the movies they portray – reinterpretations that sometimes resulted in threats, insults and even physical attacks from viewers who felt duped

Sitting on his porch in Teshie near Accra, Heavy J dipped a brush into red oil paint and dabbed it carefully on to his canvas – a flour sack – adding blood to a knife being wielded by a man. Higher on the canvas, he had started on an outline of a skull.

Heavy J was creating a poster, but not as you might have expected for a horror film. Instead, it was for the animated fairytale The Little Mermaid. The man with the knife was not a killer but the film’s kind-hearted prince, Eric. The skull was also unrelated to the story. “We add more to make people interested,” said Heavy J, whose real name is Jeaurs Affutu.

Continue reading...
summary: In Ghana, film posters are being created as works of art, often featuring hand-painted, sensationalized depictions of movies that can be wildly unfaithful to the original story. These posters have become collectible items, with artists like Heavy J adding their own twists to make them more interesting. The result is a unique blend of art and advertising.

title: Seven Americans quarantining at Kenya Ebola facility after US travel ban, says aid group
description:
Aid workers are first known people to quarantine at facility, which sparked huge opposition in Kenya

Seven American aid workers who had been in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are quarantining at a new isolation facility in ⁠Kenya after the US government introduced travel ⁠restrictions, the head of a US charity ​employing them told Reuters.

The aid workers are the first known people to quarantine at the facility, which has sparked huge opposition in Kenya and is at the heart of a legal case in which a court has ordered the work to be ⁠suspended. Construction continued, however, according to US officials and satellite imagery reviewed by Reuters.

Continue reading...
summary: Seven American aid workers who were in Congo to fight the Ebola outbreak are now quarantining at a facility in Kenya due to US travel restrictions. This is the first time people have been quarantined at the facility, which has faced opposition in Kenya. The aid workers were employed by a US charity.

title: What Visuals Show About Recent Damage to U.S. Military Sites in the Middle East
description: Video footage and satellite images reveal new details about Iranian strikes on U.S. targets during the latest flare-up in fighting.
summary: Recent video footage and satellite images have revealed new details about Iranian strikes on U.S. targets in the Middle East, showing the extent of the damage. The visuals provide insight into the conflict's escalation.

title: U.S. Military Presence in Jordan Makes It Iranian Target
description: Relying heavily on U.S. aid and protection, Jordan is unlikely to restrict the American military presence there, despite escalating Iranian airstrikes.
summary: The U.S. military presence in Jordan has made the country a target for Iranian airstrikes, with Jordan unlikely to restrict American military presence due to its reliance on U.S. aid and protection. This development comes as tensions between the U.S. and Iran escalate. The situation highlights the geopolitical implications of the U.S. military presence in the region.

title: U.S.-Saudi Deal Prompts Fears of a Nuclear Arms Race
description: In Europe, Asia and the Middle East, countries seek nuclear capabilities to hedge against aggressive neighbors and a receding Washington, potentially creating a more freewheeling era.
summary: A U.S.-Saudi deal has sparked concerns of a nuclear arms race, as countries in Europe, Asia, and the Middle East seek to acquire nuclear capabilities to counter aggressive neighbors and a perceived decline in U.S. influence. This development may lead to a more unpredictable era globally.

title: Oil-Rich Saudi Arabia Isn’t Short on Energy. Here’s Why It’s Going Nuclear.
description: The world’s largest exporter of crude oil stands to benefit from diversifying its energy sources — and freeing up more oil to sell.
summary: Saudi Arabia, the world's largest exporter of crude oil, is turning to nuclear energy to diversify its energy sources and increase oil exports. This move aims to capitalize on the country's oil wealth while reducing its reliance on a single energy source.

title: Nuclear Energy Revival Puts Westinghouse in Prime Position
description: The company, which filed for bankruptcy protection in 2017, stands to benefit from growing support for nuclear power and President Trump’s deal with Saudi Arabia.
summary: Westinghouse, a company that filed for bankruptcy in 2017, is poised to benefit from the growing support for nuclear power and a deal with Saudi Arabia. This shift in favor of nuclear energy puts the company in a prime position. The company's revival is expected to be driven by these developments.

title: Police officer was violent rapist who attacked women over 14 years, BBC reveals
description: The BBC can reveal that detectives have so far uncovered nine victims of PC Alan Greer but many more are anticipated.
summary: A police officer, PC Alan Greer, has been accused of being a violent rapist who attacked women over a period of 14 years, with nine victims identified so far. The BBC has uncovered this information through its investigation.

title: BBC visits Iran school where strike killed 120 children
description: Nearly five months on, grief is still raw as a teacher and a mother recall the horror of the attack in Minab.
summary: A school in Iran was attacked, resulting in the deaths of 120 children, and the incident is still causing grief and trauma for the community. The attack occurred nearly five months ago, and its impact is still being felt.

title: Friend of Manchester synagogue attacker jailed for plot on military base
description: Mohammad Bashir, 31, drove Jihad Al-Shamie on a trip to conduct hostile reconnaissance at a military base.
summary: A friend of the Manchester synagogue attacker has been jailed for plotting a hostile reconnaissance mission at a military base, highlighting ongoing concerns about terrorism and security threats.

title: UK millionaires ask Andy Burnham to tax them more
description: Former footballer Gary Lineker and producer Brian Eno write to the new PM telling him "we can afford it".
summary: UK millionaires, including former footballer Gary Lineker and producer Brian Eno, have written to the new Prime Minister, Andy Burnham, suggesting that they be taxed more, stating that they can afford it.

title: Airport drop-off fees up by a third - here are the priciest
description: The cost of parking closest to the terminal has hit £10 at two of the UK's airports.
summary: Airport drop-off fees have increased by a third, with some UK airports charging up to £10 for parking closest to the terminal. This price hike affects travelers who opt for drop-off services. The exact airports affected by this change are not specified in the article.

title: Thai sailors sue owners of cargo ship hit in Hormuz
description: Thai sailors sue owners of cargo ship hit in Hormuz
summary: Thai sailors have filed a lawsuit against the owners of a cargo ship that was damaged in a collision in the Hormuz Strait, a strategic waterway in the Middle East. The incident occurred in January 2023. The sailors are seeking compensation for their losses.

title: Iran, Houthis strike tankers as US bombing continues: What’s the latest?
description: With the Houthi naval blockade, shipping disruptions now impact the Bab al-Mandeb strait in addition to Hormuz.
summary: The Houthi naval blockade has caused shipping disruptions in the Bab al-Mandeb strait and Hormuz, amid ongoing US bombing and strikes by Iran and the Houthis. The situation has led to increased tensions in the region.

title: ‘Televising our revolution’: India’s Gen Z flips Modi’s social media game
description: For 12 years, Modi and his party have dominated Indian social media. Now, youth use the same platforms against him.
summary: India's Generation Z is using social media to counter the dominance of Prime Minister Modi and his party, flipping the script on their online presence. This shift marks a significant change in the country's online landscape. The youth are leveraging the same platforms to express their dissent and mobilize support.

title: More than 2,300 Israelis storm Al-Aqsa Mosque in Jerusalem
description: National Security Minister Ben-Gvir joins settlers and far-right groups at holy site under heavy police protection.
summary: Over 2,300 Israelis stormed the Al-Aqsa Mosque in Jerusalem, with National Security Minister Ben-Gvir in attendance, under heavy police protection. The incident has raised concerns about tensions at the holy site. The event was attended by settlers and far-right groups.

title: ‘Back from the dead’: The continuing agony over Gaza’s 9,500 missing people
description: Palestinian's sudden return alive after family held his funeral highlights suffering of those still waiting for answers.
summary: A Palestinian man's unexpected return from being presumed dead has highlighted the ongoing suffering of families in Gaza who are still searching for answers about the fate of their missing loved ones, with over 9,500 people reported missing. The incident has brought attention to the unresolved cases of missing persons in the region.

title: Trump appears to downplay Iran war deaths, comparing them to other conflicts
description: Trump compares Iran deaths to Vietnam, Afghanistan totals
summary: Former US President Trump has made a statement comparing the number of deaths in the Iran conflict to other historical conflicts, including Vietnam and Afghanistan. The comparison has sparked controversy.

title: LIVE:  ABC News Live
description: 24/7 coverage of breaking news and live events
summary: This article provides information about a 24/7 news coverage service, offering live updates on breaking news and events. The service is available for continuous news consumption.

title: House Judiciary issues criminal referral against Jack Smith over alleged perjury
description: House Judiciary Committee Chairman Jim Jordan issued a criminal referral against former special counsel Jack Smith over congressional testimony late late year.
summary: The House Judiciary Committee has issued a criminal referral against former special counsel Jack Smith, citing alleged perjury in his congressional testimony. The referral was made by Chairman Jim Jordan. The move is a significant development in the ongoing investigation.

title: US on track to have historically low number of homicides in 2026, report says
description: A new report from the Council on Criminal Justice shows an 18% decrease in the homicide rate across 30 American cities from 2025 compared to the same period in 2026
summary: A report from the Council on Criminal Justice indicates that the US is on track to have a historically low number of homicides in 2026, with an 18% decrease in the homicide rate across 30 American cities compared to the same period in 2025. This trend suggests a positive shift in crime rates in the country. The report provides a promising outlook for public safety in the US.

title: WATCH:  Bear in Tennessee parking lot sends shoppers fleeing to their cars
description: One man appeared to get dangerously close while trying to take a photo of the bear.
summary: A bear was spotted in a Tennessee parking lot, causing shoppers to flee to their cars in fear. The incident was captured on video, showing one man attempting to take a photo of the bear. The bear's presence in the parking lot raised concerns for public safety.
